form_analysis.form_header_text: Fiscal Year 2027 Appropriations Programmatic & Language Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Instructions: 1) Only programmatic and language requests will be considered. Community Project Funding/Earmark requests will not be considered. 2) Due date for requests are March 11th, 2026. 3) All questions on the form must be completed. 4) One submission per form. 5) Include the letter text asking for the request on the form below and send the letter on your entity's official letterhead to FL03.Appropriations@mail.house.gov. 6) Include draft letter text to the relevant Appropriations Subcommittee on the form below and send the draft letter to FL03.Appropriations@mail.house.gov. 7) All submitted forms are subject to public disclosure. 8) If you have any questions about the appropriations form, please email Matthew McCulloch (matthew.mcculloch@mail.house.gov) with subject line "Cammack, FY2027 Appropriations Form Question"

sections.description: Provide information about the organization requesting funding or language changes.

fields.label: Type of entity (non-profit, university, government, etc):
Co-sponsoring organizations (for requests that have multiple sponsors):

sections.description: Information for the primary person managing this request.
Optional third-party or additional contact information.
General summary of the programmatic or language request.

fields.label: Type of Request:
fields.rationale: This is a request for a specific funding amount ($9 billion) for an existing agency account (NASA Science Mission Directorate), which is defined as a programmatic request in the reference values.

fields.label: Suggest an official program or language title for the request:
fields.rationale: This is the standard programmatic title for the account as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: Suggest an official clear and simple 1-sentence description:
fields.draft_value: This request provides $9 billion for NASA's Science Mission Directorate to advance U.S. space leadership, support critical research at institutions like the University of Florida, and maintain our competitive edge.
fields.rationale: Combines the specific funding ask with local impact data (University of Florida) and strategic talking points regarding national leadership.

sections.description: Complete this section if you selected 'Programmatic' as the request type.

fields.label: Subcommittee (ex: Commerce, Justice, Science):
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: Identified in the reference values as the correct subcommittee for NASA funding.

fields.label: Agency (ex: Commerce Department):
fields.rationale: The parent agency for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Account (ex: Bureau of the Census):
fields.rationale: The specific budget account name for the Science Mission Directorate within NASA.

fields.label: Amount requested:
fields.rationale: The exact funding ask amount provided in the reference values.

fields.label: Amount included in President's Budget (if relevant):
fields.rationale: The FY2027 President's Budget Request amount is currently unknown as per the reference data.

fields.label: Amount increase/decrease from President's Budget (if relevant):
fields.rationale: Calculation is impossible because the President's Budget Request amount is unknown.

fields.label: FY26 appropriated (if relevant):
fields.help_text: Historical appropriation amount for fiscal year 2026.
fields.rationale: Directly uses the pre-filled value confirmed by the FY2026 enacted data in the reference values.

fields.label: Amount increase/decrease over FY26:
fields.rationale: The difference between the $9B request and the $7.25B FY2026 enacted amount.

sections.description: Complete this section if you selected 'Language' as the request type.

fields.label: Subcommittee (ex: Commerce, Justice, Science):
fields.rationale: This appears to be a duplicate field for a different request type (Language); the reference values indicate this is a programmatic request.

fields.label: Agency (ex: Commerce Department):
fields.rationale: This appears to be a duplicate field for a different request type (Language); the reference values indicate this is a programmatic request.

fields.label: Account (ex: Bureau of the Census):
fields.rationale: This appears to be a duplicate field for a different request type (Language); the reference values indicate this is a programmatic request.

fields.label: Bill or the Report language request:
fields.rationale: Reference values explicitly state 'N/A' for Bill and Report Language requests.

fields.label: Exact�requested language:
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic funding request for the Science Mission Directorate; no specific legislative or report language is being requested at this time.

fields.label:
Has this language ever been included in a subcommittee's bill?�

If so, please cite the fiscal year, bill number, division, title, section, and page number:
fields.rationale: This request is programmatic in nature and does not involve specific bill or report language.

sections.description: Provide context on the request's priority and benefit.

fields.label: Priority Number�(If submitting multiple requests):
fields.rationale: Priority is user-specific and cannot be determined from the provided context.

fields.label: List all Members of the House and Senate who are receiving this request:
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request. Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

fields.label: Description of language or programmatic request�(500 words or less):
fields.draft_value:
* $9,000,000,000 for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) to fund Earth Science, Planetary Science, Astrophysics, Heliophysics, and Biological and Physical Sciences
* Implementation of National Academies' Decadal Survey priorities for rigorously vetted, high-impact scientific discovery
* Restoration of funding stability to protect the aerospace workforce and ensure the continuity of multi-year mission development cycles
fields.rationale: Directly cites the $9 billion SMD funding ask and priority areas defined in the reference context.

fields.label: How this language or programmatic request will help your organization, community, Florida, and/or the nation�(150 words or less):
fields.draft_value:
* Sustains $44.8 million in recent NASA Science obligations in FL-03 (FY23-25), supporting major local projects including a $15,706,435 contract at the University of Florida for critical LISA mission hardware
* Funds Gainesville-based research into ocean world habitability ($2.9M grant) and advanced gravitational referencing technology ($11,999,943 grant)
* Maintains the local STEM talent pipeline and ensures U.S. strategic leadership in the global space race against competitors like China
fields.rationale: Cites specific local spending totals and University of Florida award data from the NASA spending context.

fields.label: Why would federal resources used to carry out this programmatic/language request be an appropriate use of taxpayer resources�(150 words or less):
fields.draft_value:
* High Return on Investment: Every $1 invested in NASA returns approximately $3 to the American economy through innovation and industrial demand
* Essential National Security: Advances U.S. leadership and sets exploration norms as international competitors rapidly expand their space science capabilities
* Tangible Public Benefits: Delivers critical data for weather forecasting, public health risk modeling, and planetary defense to protect American lives and property
fields.rationale: Draws on economic impact data and strategic leadership talking points provided in the advocacy context.

sections.description: Provide the text of required letters. Reminder: PDF copies must also be emailed to FL03.Appropriations@mail.house.gov.

fields.label: Please include the text of the letter for the programmatic or language request below. In addition, please send the letter PDF to FL03.Appropriations@mail.house.gov.�
fields.draft_value:
* Formal request for $9,000,000,000 for the NASA Science Mission Directorate (SMD) in the FY2027 CJS Appropriations bill
* Highlights $44.8 million in recent local impact at the University of Florida, including major hardware development for the LISA mission
* Justifies investment based on maintaining the U.S. aerospace workforce and strategic advantage in the face of increasing international competition
* Emphasizes adherence to peer-reviewed Decadal Survey priorities to ensure high-value scientific returns for the taxpayer
fields.rationale: Summarizes the $9B request and local UF spending context as key pillars for a formal request letter.

fields.label: Please include the text of a draft letter to the relevant Appropriations Subcommittee below. In addition, please send the letter PDF to FL03.Appropriations@mail.house.gov.
fields.draft_value:
* Formal request for $9,000,000,000 for the NASA Science Mission Directorate (SMD) in the FY2027 CJS Appropriations bill
* Highlights $44.8 million in recent local impact at the University of Florida, including major hardware development for the LISA mission
* Justifies investment based on maintaining the U.S. aerospace workforce and strategic advantage in the face of increasing international competition
* Emphasizes adherence to peer-reviewed Decadal Survey priorities to ensure high-value scientific returns for the taxpayer
fields.rationale: Summarizes the $9B request and local UF spending context as key pillars for a formal request letter.

top_contracts.description: Laser Interferometer Space Antenna (LISA) Charge Management Device (CMD).  The contractor shall design, fabricate, align, test, verify per approved plan and deliver a trl-6 technology maturation breadboard for the lisa mission.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Developing critical hardware for the LISA gravitational wave detector
Providing specialized technical consulting services for NASA.

top_grants.description: Gravitational Reference Advanced Technology Test In Space (GRATTIS)
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Testing advanced space technology for precise gravitational referencing.

top_grants.description: Above & below: astrobiological investigation of biosignatures emblematic of life on ocean worlds
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Investigating signs of life on distant ocean worlds.

top_grants.description: e014042-assimilation of Earth observations to improve and enhance global predictive ability of forecasting risk of cholera outbreaks
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Improving global forecasting of cholera outbreak risks.

top_grants.description: The proposal project is to develop operational concepts and deployment tests to enable the genomics and gene expression analyses associated with suborbital flights. The project will conduct the first human tended  suborbital whole genome gene expression experiments  using plants as the test organisms and using the technology of the kennedy space center fixation tubes (KFTS) for safety containment and operational effectiveness. The basic science is straightforward in that changes in gene expression are well characterized between being in orbit on ISS and on Earth  but no science has yet been done to address changes in gene expression during transition to and from microgravity. The technology of the kfts has been well developed for many spaceflight and spaceflight related operational environments however they have never been deployed in suborbital vehicles nor have the associated operations plans or associated facilities been developed for suborbital deployments. The concept of operations is simple: - 12 kfts will be loaded preflight with plants in the sample volume and rnalater preservative in the fixative volume. This keeps each replicate of the experiment safely and operationally containerized. - the kfts will be carried in a tbd personal bag or within the flight suit of the participant flyer  or stowed in the vehicle in a location tbd. - at each of four key stages in the flight (1g before boost  beginning of zero g  end of zero g  and then at the end of flight) 3 kfts will be activated to mix the rnalater with the plants  essentially capturing the gene expression occurring at the time of activation. - after activation the kfts will stored in a tbd location for return. - all analysis will be post flight in the laboratories of the PI team. Kfts have been used on the space shuttle  the ISS and in several flight environments including parabolic flights on kc-135  c-9  b-272 and f104 aircraft. The kfts provide all necessary levels of containment and are robust  shuttle and ISS flight tested hardware. They are easy to use  and take less than 20 seconds to actuate. The technology development goal is to examine and test the effectiveness and limitations of deploying kfts within commercial suborbital vehicles and during typical suborbital flight profiles. The scientific goal is to capture gene expression data from plants at various stages of the flight in order to characterize the rapid and early phases of the adaptation processes that accompany transitions among g-loads. This area of science  adaptation processes in response to transitional g loads to and from microgravity  is completely unknown and unapproached. This situation leaves a large gap in knowledge regarding the changes that occur in biology as organisms adapt to and from microgravity. That gap in knowledge can be richly filled using suborbital vehicles  and ca best be filled using tools and technologies that can be deployed by humans within the vehicle  thereby keeping the experiment operations similar to Earth bound and ISS experiments.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Capturing early plant gene expression changes during suborbital flight transitions.

top_grants.description: A novel method using an Electrostatic Levitator (EL) is proposed to determine the interfacial tension (IFT) of liquid/gas systems. This key thermophysical property is of interest for materials processing and in welding operations in space and on ground. The novel method for measurement of ift proposed involves the use of electrostatically forced faraday instability. In this method  an oscillatory Electrostatic forcing is applied to a liquid system  resulting in sudden pattern formation at the interface  i.E.  An instability is caused. The "instability" is a result of resonance between the imposed forcing frequency and the natural frequency of the system. The critical amplitude and mode shapes are predictable from linear stability theory and agree superbly with experiments. The measurement of ift directly aligns with the goals of the thermophysical property measurement emphasis of the current nra. Faraday experiments by this team using mechanical forcing have shown excellent agreement between theory and experimental results. However  in the case of mechanical forcing  the force of gravity overwhelms the effects of ift on the system stability and therefore it is almost impossible to determine the ift with accuracy. Electrostatic forcing counters gravity  allowing the effects of ift to play a significant role in the ultimate determination of the system stability. Accordingly  experimentally mapped stability curves can be fit accurately with theoretical curves  allowing for the determination of the ift with great accuracy. The proposing team has successfully used Electrostatic forcing in ground equipment to show that faraday waves can be obtained as per theoretical predictions. The current state of the art uses an open container geometry to hold the liquid. However  a levitated sphere may also be used  and our proposal necessarily includes this method. We therefore propose a project with two new aspects. In the first stage of the research  we propose to use ground-based facilities at JAXA and NASA marshall to accurately obtain the ift for liquids using Electrostatic faraday forcing in open containers as well as in levitated drops. In the second stage of the research  we will perform flight experiments using the elf in ISS to obtain the threshold for instability and determine the ift of these systems. In summary  the research allows an alternate new method  viz.  Faraday instability  for the determination of interfacial tension. Using Electrostatic forcing instability will permit an independent confirmation of measurements obtained by the single-pulse oscillatingdrop technique where decay rates are measured and related to interfacial tension and viscosity. Ground-based research using the Electrostatic Levitator available at the NASA MSFC and JAXA  and space experiments on elf in the ISS will provide accurate data. In addition to being an alternative method for ift measurement  the proposed technique also has versatility as a potential means in measuring interfacial tension for high viscosity materials and two-liquid interfacial tension on ground as well as in space. The proposing team has recently collaborated successfully with JAXA and has conducted experiments to study the feasibility of Electrostatic faraday instability. These results form the basis for the current proposal.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Accurately measuring liquid interfacial tension for space materials processing.
